_comment: STAGING SOURCE — Oslo Day 1/2 stops with researched 2026 opening hours and open/closed logic. NOT deployed (see .assetsignore). To be merged into the encrypted map once the trip password is available. Coordinates are approximate landmark centroids; verify before final render. Hours researched 2026-06-29.
openClosedLogic: A stop is OPEN now if (a) alwaysOpen=true, OR (b) today's weekday falls in openDays AND current time is within hours for that day AND (season==null OR today within season). Stops with bookingRequired or seasonal interiors should render an info badge even when grounds are open.

stops.name: Karl Johans gate
stops.note: Main pedestrian artery linking Central Station to the Palace; shops typically 10:00–18:00.

stops.name: Oslo Royal Palace (Det kongelige slott)
stops.note: Park always open & free. Interior = pre-booked guided tours via Ticketmaster, 20 Jun–16 Aug 2026 only (sells out). Changing of the Guard daily 13:30.

stops.name: Oslo Rådhus (City Hall)
stops.note: Free entry; airport-style security. Can close for events (e.g. ~10 Dec Nobel Prize).

stops.name: Nobels Fredssenter (Nobel Peace Center)
stops.hours: Mid-Jun–Aug: daily 10:00–17:00 | Jan–mid-Jun: Tue & Thu–Sun 11:00–17:00, Wed 11:00–21:00
stops.warning: CLOSED MONDAYS (year-round outside peak). Confirm if Day 1 is a Monday.

stops.name: Nasjonalmuseet (National Museum)
stops.warning: CLOSED MONDAYS. Large museum — allow 2–3 hrs.

stops.name: Aker Brygge
stops.note: Lunch + afternoon stroll. Seaport dinner options here & at Tjuvholmen next door.

stops.name: Akershus Festning (Fortress)
stops.note: Grounds free & open daily. Castle interior + Resistance Museum have shorter, restricted hours.

stops.name: Munchmuseet (MUNCH)
stops.note: Open daily (exc. 1 May). Best EVENING stop — open to 21:00 Wed–Sun. The '+/-' optional item; recommend prioritising over Opera foyer for the night slot.

stops.name: Oslo Opera House (Operahuset)
stops.note: Reopened after maintenance 15 Jan 2026. Foyer closes 16:00 — by 'night' only the roof walk is available (still worth it).

stops.name: Deichman Bjørvika (Public Library)
stops.note: Striking architecture, open late — good evening stop near the Opera.

stops.name: Seaport dinner
stops.note: Options: Aker Brygge / Tjuvholmen, Vippetangen, or Barcode (Bjørvika) near Opera.

stops.name: Bygdøy ferry (B9)
stops.note: From Rådhusbrygge 3 (by City Hall) to Bygdøy museums. SEASONAL Apr–Oct only. Covered by Ruter ticket / boat ticket.

stops.name: Norsk Folkemuseum
stops.note: Open-air folk museum. In summer open daily incl. Mondays.

stops.name: Fram Museum (Frammuseet)
stops.note: Open every day all year. Polar exploration ship Fram.

stops.name: Kon-Tiki Museum
stops.note: Next door to Fram. Open daily.

stops.name: Cafe near Bygdøynes
stops.note: Museum cafes at Fram/Kon-Tiki/Maritime Museum cluster on Bygdøynes.

stops.name: Vigeland Sculpture Park (Frognerparken)
stops.note: Always open & free. ~15 min from Bygdøy / central Oslo.

stops.name: Dinner
stops.note: Near Frogner/Majorstuen after the park, or back to centre.

feasibilitySummary: Day 1 should NOT be a Monday — Nasjonalmuseet AND Nobel Peace Center are both closed Mondays year-round (all other Day-1 stops are Monday-fine).
Royal Palace interior needs pre-booked Ticketmaster tour tickets and only runs 20 Jun–16 Aug 2026; otherwise exterior + 13:30 guard change only.
MUNCH (to 21:00 Wed–Sun) is the best evening stop; Opera foyer closes 16:00 so by night only its roof walk is available.
Day 2 is clean in summer: all three Bygdøy museums open daily and the B9 ferry runs Apr–Oct. Confirm trip dates fall in that window.
Day 1 morning is ambitious (5 stops incl. a 2–3 hr museum) — realistically Nasjonalmuseet OR a deep Nobel visit before lunch, not both.
